title: Inventory Simplified Launcher Surface
summary: Simplified launcher surface inventory for the vanilla parity rebuild. Records the entrypoint at src/main.ts, the four-file launcher module directory under src/launcher/, the public TypeScript surface those modules expose, the CLI flags and defaults the simplified launcher accepts, and the FFI surface src/launcher/win32.ts dlopens. Confirms the simplified launcher is not the vanilla D_DoomMain startup path that the canonical bun run doom.ts target must reproduce.
evidence_method: direct local repository audit performed by the inventory lane: ls of src/launcher/, sha256sum and wc -l over src/main.ts and the four src/launcher/*.ts files, ripgrep over the launcher modules to enumerate exported symbols and CLI parameter names, and ripgrep across src/ for D_DoomMain and D_DoomLoop references.

launcher_entrypoint.drives_via: void main().catch in src/main.ts; main() awaits runLauncherWindow(session, options) from src/launcher/win32.ts after createLauncherSession from src/launcher/session.ts.

vanilla_d_doom_main_path_status.notes: src/main.ts and src/launcher/* implement a simplified gameplay-only launcher: parse CLI, load WAD resources via loadLauncherResources, build a LauncherSession via createLauncherSession, and drive runLauncherWindow which presents indexed frames via Win32 StretchDIBits. The vanilla D_DoomMain pre-loop sequence (initialTryRunTics, restoreBuffer, executeSetViewSize, startGameLoop) and per-frame phase ordering modeled by src/mainLoop.ts are not invoked by this launcher. Title loop, intermission, finale, save/load, and demo playback paths are intentionally absent from the simplified surface.

implications: src/launcher/ owns the only currently runnable wiring; the canonical bun run doom.ts entrypoint declared by plan_vanilla_parity/establish-vanilla-parity-control-center.md must replace this surface, not extend it.

The simplified launcher exposes nine interfaces (GameplayRenderContext, GameplayRenderResources, GameplayRenderState, GameplayTexture, LauncherInputState, LauncherResources, LauncherSession, LauncherSessionOptions, LauncherWindowOptions), eight functions (advanceLauncherSession, createGameplayRenderState, createLauncherSession, loadGameplayRenderResources, loadLauncherResources, renderGameplayFrame, renderLauncherFrame, runLauncherWindow), and one constant (EMPTY_LAUNCHER_INPUT) that downstream parity steps must keep stable until the launch lane swaps in the vanilla startup path.

The simplified launcher accepts six CLI surface flags (--help/-help/-h, --iwad, --list-maps, --map, --scale, --skill) and three default constants (DEFAULT_MAP_NAME=E1M1, DEFAULT_SCALE=2, DEFAULT_SKILL=2) which differ from vanilla DOOM's command-line surface and must not be treated as a parity contract.

src/launcher/win32.ts dlopens user32.dll and gdi32.dll directly with thirteen Win32 symbols (twelve user32 plus StretchDIBits) and depends on src/host/win32/* and src/host/windowPolicy.ts/ticAccumulator.ts; the launch lane's vanilla replacement must preserve these FFI surfaces or migrate them in lockstep with their host module dependencies.

The simplified launcher does not call D_DoomMain or D_DoomLoop and bypasses src/mainLoop.ts; downstream lanes that rely on the vanilla phase ordering must not assume the simplified launcher exercises those phases.
